option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"/Users/gabrieluwaila/IdeaProjects/Portfolio":"{\"/index.html\":\"This file is the index.html file for a portfolio website. Some key things it does:\\n\\n- Defines the basic HTML structure with DOCTYPE, html, head and body tags\\n- Sets the page title, links CSS stylesheets and JS files\\n- Contains the main page content sections:\\n - Header with logo and navbar \\n - Welcome section with intro text and profile pic\\n - Empty sections for Projects, About Me and Contact that will be populated with content\\n- Includes footer with social media links\\n\\nKey functions:\\n\\n- Defines the overall page layout and sections\\n- Links necessary CSS and JS files \\n- Provides navigation between main sections via navbar\\n- Displays intro text and profile image on welcome page\\n- Sets up empty containers for project, about and contact sections that will be filled out\\n- Includes footer with external links like GitHub, LinkedIn etc\\n\\nSo in summary, it sets up the basic template and structure for the portfolio site with all the necessary page elements like navigation, sections etc. Individual sections will then be populated with actual content.\",\"/js/app.js\":\"Based on the limited information provided, here is what I can infer about the js/app.js file:\\n\\n- It is a JavaScript file that is likely the main application code file. The .js extension indicates it contains JavaScript code. \\n\\n- The location in a js folder suggests it contains JavaScript code related to the application/website rather than general utilities. \\n\\n- The name \\\"app\\\" implies this file contains the core application logic and initialization code.\\n\\n- Key functions it likely contains:\\n\\n - Initialization/bootstrapping code to launch the app\\n\\n - Event handlers to respond to user interactions\\n\\n - Modules/classes to encapsulate different parts of the app logic\\n\\n - Utilities/helpers for common tasks\\n\\n - Data models to represent app data\\n\\n - Routing/navigation logic to control app flows/views\\n\\n - AJAX/data fetching code to load/save remote data\\n\\n - Error/exception handling\\n\\n - Testing/debugging utilities\\n\\n - Build/bundling configuration if used in a larger project\\n\\nSo in summary, js/app.js is likely the main JavaScript file that initializes and coordinates the core logic and functionality of the application. Without seeing the actual code contents, these are some educated guesses about its purpose and key responsibilities based on conventions.\",\"/README.md\":\"This README.md file serves as documentation for a portfolio project. Some key things it likely does:\\n\\n- Provides an overview and description of the portfolio project - what it is, its purpose, features, etc. This gives readers an introduction and context.\\n\\n- Lists instructions on how to set up and run the portfolio locally - things like dependencies, installation steps, how to start a development server, etc. This helps users get started with the project.\\n\\n- Describes the overall structure and organization of the portfolio codebase - folder structure, key files and what they contain, overall architecture. This helps orient new contributors. \\n\\n- Documents any build tools, libraries, frameworks used and how they integrate. For example build systems like Webpack, programming languages, CSS frameworks, etc. \\n\\n- Contains guidelines, standards and best practices for developing the portfolio - coding style, commit messages, testing procedures. \\n\\n- Acts as the central source of documentation - it and any other docs files are the first place to look to understand the portfolio project.\\n\\n- May include roadmap, contribution guidelines and other information for outside contributors.\\n\\nSo in summary, a README helps introduce, explain and provide guidance on working with the portfolio project for both end users and other developers. It serves as the starting point for understanding.\"}"}
